prompt: Thousands of surveillance cameras mounted on a massive brutalist obelisk, all pointing at a single person below. The person is holding a small phone, voluntarily broadcasting their location. Behind the obelisk, rows of identical grey confinement units stretch to the horizon. Harsh fluorescent lighting. No shadows — everything is exposed. 70s dystopian illustration. The feeling is: they built the surveillance system themselves, then wondered why it watched them.

Create an image with a fun retro scientific illustration style.
Additional art direction: 1970s dystopian science fiction illustration. Style of HR Giger meets Soviet propaganda meets John Harris. Industrial decay, oppressive architecture, toxic skies. Desaturated palette with sickly reds, greys, and toxic yellows. Claustrophobic, decaying, surveillance-state aesthetic. No text, no labels, no UI elements.
